page.title: Complex Passive Impersonal Style
page.subtitle_html: Complex Passive Impersonal Style - C2 impersonal passive and reporting-passive patterns.
page.subtitle_text: C2 impersonal passive and reporting-passive patterns.

blocks.body: {"level":"C2","intro":"Complex passive and impersonal style: compact anchors for formal reporting structures with it, personal passive subjects, and perfect infinitives.","rules":[{"label":"Impersonal passive","text":"Use It is believed that, It is reported that, and similar forms to present claims without naming the source.","example":"It is believed that this policy reduces risk."},{"label":"Personal passive","text":"Move the reported subject forward with is said to, is thought to, or is expected to.","example":"The policy is believed to reduce risk."},{"label":"Perfect passive reporting","text":"Use passive perfect infinitives when the reported action happened earlier.","example":"The errors are reported to have been found."}]}

blocks.heading: C2 focus
blocks.body: This compact page anchors the Polyglot English C2 lesson on complex passive and impersonal style.
